text: HE et al.: AUTOMATICALLY IDENTIFYING CVE AFFECTED VERSIONS WITH PATCHES AND DEVELOPER LOGS 919

Yongzhong He received the PhD degree in com- puter science from the Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences, in 2006. He is a full professor with the School of Computer, Beijing Jiaotong Uni- versity, China. He did research with Oakland Univer- sity, in 2009, and the Pennsylvania State University during 2014-2015 as a visiting scholar. His research interests include system security, privacy protection, and mobile security.
Yiming Wang received the M.S. degree in cy- berspace security from Beijing Jiaotong Univer- sity, in 2022. His research interest includes system security.

Sencun Zhu received the PhD degree in information technology from George Mason University, in 2004. He is an associate professor with Penn State Univer- sity. His research interests include privacy, wireless and mobile security, smartphone security, anomaly detection, IoT security, and ML/DL related security.

Wei Wang received the PhD degree in control science and engineering from Xi’an Jiaotong University, in 2006. He is currently a full professor with the School of Computer, Beijing Jiaotong University, China. His main research interests include mobile, computer, and network security.
YunjiaZhang receivedtheM.S.degreeincyberspace security from Beijing Jiaotong University, in 2021. Her research interest includes system security.

Qiang Li received the PhD degree in computer sci- ence from the University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, in 2015. Currently, he is an associate pro- fessor with the School of Computer and Information Technology, Beijing Jiaotong University, China. His research interests include revolve around Internet of Things, software security, artiﬁcial intelligence, and machine learning for cybersecurity
Aimin Yu is a research scientist with the Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sci- ences. His research interests include system security and trust computing.
